title: Be careful where you click — ChatGPT joins Microsoft, Google in most-impersonated brands online
content_html:
• OpenAI now accounts for 1.1% of all tracked brand impersonations, ranking top 10
  • Microsoft and LinkedIn still account for a joint 34.2% of all impersonations
  • Basic cybersecurity hygiene can prevent many attacks

New research from Check Point has revealed ChatGPT is becoming increasingly targeted in brand phishing attempts, with the company now appearing in the top-10 list alongside heavy hitters like Microsoft, Google and Apple.

Though ChatGPT only accounted for 1.1% of all tracked brand phishing attempts, this marks the first time it's appeared in the top-10 and reflects continued growth for attackers.

It sees a similar number of attacks to PayPal (1.3%), WhatsApp (1.4%) and Facebook (1.9%), but at present, it's far behind Microsoft, which when combined with its LinkedIn subsidiary, accounts for more than a third (34.2%) of all tracked brand impersonations.

ChatGPT is growing as an impersonated brand

One example from the second quarter of this year saw fake ChatGPT Plus payment failure emails copying OpenAI's branding, but directing victims to a fraudulent payment page to maliciously collect their payment information.

As for Microsoft, fake support pages warned customers that they needed to update Office for security fixes, but the download actually installed malware on victim devices.

Attacks on OpenAI are fairly ironic, because it's likely to company's own AI tools (among plenty of others) that actually helped attackers to write many of the attacks at lightning pace.

Overall, tech companies were targeted most, followed by social media platforms in a similar vein, and then banking apps.

Sign up to the TechRadar Pro newsletter to get all the top news, opinion, features and guidance your business needs to succeed!

Despite fluctuations in terms of which brands are targeted and how campaigns look, the general attack vector remains unchanged, with cybercriminals targeting vulnerable users and emphasizing urgency to trick people out of sharing information, credentials and payment details.

Security experts warn potential victims to be weary of clicking on unknown URLs and opening unexpected communications, as well as to protect their accounts with passkeys and secure multi-factor authentication (MFA).

content_html_zh:
• OpenAI 目前占所有跟踪品牌模仿的 1.1%，排名前 10 名
  • 微软和 LinkedIn 仍合计占所有冒充行为的 34.2%
  • 基本的网络安全卫生可以防止许多攻击

Check Point 的最新研究显示，ChatGPT 越来越成为品牌网络钓鱼攻击的目标，该公司目前与微软、谷歌和苹果等重量级公司一起出现在前 10 名名单中。

尽管 ChatGPT 仅占所有跟踪的品牌网络钓鱼尝试的 1.1%，但这标志着它首次出现在前 10 名中，并反映出攻击者的持续增长。

它看到的攻击数量与 PayPal (1.3%)、WhatsApp (1.4%) 和 Facebook (1.9%) 类似，但目前远远落后于微软，微软与其子公司 LinkedIn 合计占所有被跟踪品牌假冒事件的三分之一以上 (34.2%)。

ChatGPT 正在成长为一个模仿品牌

今年第二季度的一个例子是，假冒的 ChatGPT Plus 付款失败电子邮件复制了 OpenAI 的品牌，但将受害者引导至欺诈性付款页面，以恶意收集他们的付款信息。

至于微软，虚假的支持页面警告客户他们需要更新 Office 以进行安全修复，但下载的内容实际上在受害者设备上安装了恶意软件。

对 OpenAI 的攻击相当讽刺，因为公司自己的 AI 工具（以及许多其他工具）很可能实际上帮助攻击者以闪电般的速度编写了许多攻击。

总体而言，科技公司是最受攻击的目标，其次是类似的社交媒体平台，然后是银行应用程序。

订阅 TechRadar Pro 时事通讯，获取您的企业成功所需的所有热门新闻、观点、功能和指导！

尽管针对的品牌和活动的外观有所不同，但总体攻击向量保持不变，网络犯罪分子以易受攻击的用户为目标，并强调诱骗人们分享信息、凭证和支付详细信息的紧迫性。

安全专家警告潜在受害者不要点击未知 URL 和打开意外通信，并使用密钥和安全多重身份验证 (MFA) 来保护他们的帐户。
